title: PUNLMP
pairSummary: Low power shows delicate papillary architecture; high power shows the minimal cytologic atypia that separates PUNLMP from overt carcinoma.
description: Wikimedia Commons H&E image showing an orderly papillary urothelial neoplasm with delicate fibrovascular cores and minimal cytologic atypia.
teachingPoint: At low power, PUNLMP retains architectural order with slender papillae and lacks the crowded, disorganized fusion pattern of high-grade papillary carcinoma.

title: PUNLMP
pairSummary: Low power shows delicate papillary architecture; high power shows the minimal cytologic atypia that separates PUNLMP from overt carcinoma.
description: Wikimedia Commons H&E image showing preserved polarity and only minimal nuclear enlargement in papillary urothelial neoplasm of low malignant potential.
teachingPoint: At high power, PUNLMP shows only mild nuclear enlargement and largely preserved polarity rather than the pleomorphism and mitotic activity expected in carcinoma.

title: Urothelial Carcinoma In Situ
pairSummary: Low-to-intermediate power establishes a flat atypical process; high power confirms full-thickness disorder and cytologic severity.
description: Wikimedia Commons H&E image showing a flat urothelial lesion with diffuse atypia and loss of maturation consistent with carcinoma in situ.
teachingPoint: Bladder CIS is a flat, high-grade lesion. The architecture is not papillary; the key is diffuse atypia with loss of maturation across the urothelial thickness.

title: Urothelial Carcinoma In Situ
pairSummary: Low-to-intermediate power establishes a flat atypical process; high power confirms full-thickness disorder and cytologic severity.
description: Wikimedia Commons H&E image emphasizing nuclear pleomorphism, hyperchromasia, and loss of polarity in urothelial carcinoma in situ.
teachingPoint: The high-power CIS view is the slide to use against reactive atypia: nuclei are enlarged, pleomorphic, hyperchromatic, and disordered through the full thickness.

title: BCG-Induced Granulomatous Cystitis
pairSummary: Treatment-related granulomatous inflammation can distract from the neoplastic question; interpret it with specimen context and prior therapy.
description: Wikimedia Commons H&E image showing treatment-related granulomatous inflammation after intravesical BCG therapy.
teachingPoint: BCG therapy can produce granulomatous cystitis that looks dramatic at low power. Always correlate with treatment history before overcalling infection or new tumor.

title: BCG-Induced Granulomatous Cystitis
pairSummary: Treatment-related granulomatous inflammation can distract from the neoplastic question; interpret it with specimen context and prior therapy.
description: Wikimedia Commons H&E image showing epithelioid histiocytes and giant-cell-rich inflammation in BCG-induced granulomatous cystitis.
teachingPoint: On higher power, the lesion reads as granulomatous inflammation rather than urothelial atypia. This is a useful counterexample when discussing post-therapy specimens.

title: Inverted Urothelial Papilloma
description: Wikimedia Commons H&E image showing a benign endophytic urothelial proliferation typical of inverted urothelial papilloma.
teachingPoint: Inverted urothelial papilloma is a benign endophytic lesion. It earns a place in the bladder lecture because it helps frame the difference between orderly inverted growth and infiltrative nests.

title: Malakoplakia
description: Wikimedia Commons H&E image showing sheets of histiocytes in malakoplakia, a classic inflammatory mimic in the urinary tract.
teachingPoint: Malakoplakia belongs in the bladder mimic slide because it can produce a mass-forming lesion and is defined by histiocytic sheets with Michaelis-Gutmann bodies.
